definitions: A long, stout pole, typically 6 to 9 feet (1.8 to 2.7 meters) in length, used as a weapon or for self-defense.
A traditional European pole weapon, often made of oak or ash, used in martial arts and historical combat.
examples: He wielded the quarterstaff with surprising agility.
The knight trained with a heavy quarterstaff.
Learning to fight with a quarterstaff was part of his education.
